request.question: Which district was Miss Universe 2013 held in?

title: Miss Universe 2013
Miss Dominican Republic 2014
Miss Universe Spain 2014
Marilyn Ramos
Olivia Wells
Puteri Indonesia 2013
Crocus City Hall
Miss Universe Spain 2013
Gabriela Isler
Lucia Aldana
sentences: Miss Universe 2013, the 62nd Miss Universe pageant, was held on 9 November 2013 at the Crocus City Hall, Krasnogorsk, a suburb of Moscow, Russia.
Olivia Culpo of the United States crowned her successor Gabriela Isler of Venezuela at the end of the event.
86 contestants competed for the crown.
Miss República Dominicana 2014 was held on August 17, 2014, in Teatro Nacional Eduardo Brito, Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic.
Miss Dominican Republic 2013 Yaritza Reyes crowned her successor, Kimberly Castillo, as Miss Dominican Republic 2014 who will represent the Dominican Republic at Miss Universe 2014.
The 1st Runner-Up or Miss Hispanoamericana Dominicana is Aletxa Mueses who will represent the country at Reina Hispanoamericana 2014.
The 2nd Runner-Up or is Dhío Moreno who will represent the country at Miss Intercontinental 2014.
The 3rd Runner-Up was Maria del Mar López who will compete in Miss Model of the World 2014.
The 4th Runner-Up was Analíz de la Cruz who will compete in Miss Globe International 2014.
Miss Universe 2013 Gabriela Isler assisted in the crowning of Miss Dominican Republic 2014.
Miss Spain Universe 2014 was the second edition of the Miss Universe Spain beauty pageant.
It was held on October 28, 2014 at the Teatro Bodevil in the city of Madrid.
Patricia Yurena Rodríguez, Miss Spain Universe 2013, crowned the winner Desirée Cordero Ferrer as her successor who represented Spain in the Miss Universe 2014.

Marilyn Ramos (born 16 February 1991) is a South African designer, model and beauty pageant titleholder who was crowned Miss South Africa 2012, becoming the official representative of her country to Miss Universe 2013 and Miss World 2013.
Ramos represented South Africa at Miss World 2013 and Miss Universe 2013, failing to place in both competitions.

Olivia Genevieve Wells (born 29 April 1994 in Melbourne) is an Australian charity worker and beauty pageant titleholder who was crowned Miss Universe Australia 2013 and represented Australia at Miss Universe 2013 in Moscow, Russia on 9 November 2013.
She is the first woman to be crowned Miss Universe Australia from the state of Victoria.
Puteri Indonesia 2012-2013, the 17th Annual Puteri Indonesia beauty pageant, was held in Jakarta Convention Center, Jakarta, Indonesia on February 1, 2013.

Previous edition of Puteri Indonesia was held on October 2011 and the next edition was supposed to be held on 2012, but because Miss Universe 2012 was held in December 2012 the organization decided to reschedule the event to February 2013.
Thirty eight contestants from all 33 provinces of Indonesia competed for the title of Puteri Indonesia, one of the most prominent beauty pageant titles in the country.
Maria Selena, Puteri Indonesia 2011 from Central Java crowned her successor Whulandary Herman from West Sumatera at the end of this event.

The winner is going to represent Indonesia at the Miss Universe 2013, while the runners-up will represent the nation at the Miss International 2013, Miss Supranational 2013 and Miss Grand International 2013.
The event was broadcast live on Indonesian television network, Indosiar.
The winner of Miss Universe 2012 Olivia Culpo from USA, was present during the event.

Crocus City Hall (Russian: Крокус Сити Холл ) is a concert hall administratively located in "Krasnogorsk Urban Settlement" (near the city of Krasnogorsk) in Krasnogorsky District, Moscow Oblast (north-west to Moscow Ring Road).
Miss Universe Spain 2013 was the very first edition of the Miss Universe Spain pageant.

It was held in the Convention Center at the Hotel Ada Palace in Madrid on 11 September 2013, exclusively for selecting the Spanish representative to Miss Universe 2013 as a result of the discontinuation of the traditional Miss Spain pageant which was last held in 2011.
María Gabriela de Jesús Isler Morales (born March 21, 1988) is a Venezuelan TV Host, fashion model and beauty queen who was crowned Miss Universe 2013 in Moscow, Russia.
Isler also crowned Miss Venezuela 2012.
she is the seventh Miss Universe from Venezuela and the third Miss Universe from Venezuela in six years.

Gabriela is currently the Founder and CEO of the Universe of Blessings Fund, a 501(c)3 organization devoted to the empowerment of young women and girls by providing critical life-skills and personal development counseling.
Carmen Lucía Aldana Roldán (born March 9, 1992 in Cali) is a Colombian model and holds the title of Miss Colombia 2012.
Aldana represented Colombia at Miss Universe 2013.
She was the last Colombian delegate of the Miss Universe 1st Colombian drought, as her successor as Miss Colombia, Paulina Vega, won the title as Miss Universe 2014.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Disambiguation for short/ambiguous title tokens:
- If an anchor in summary_1 is a short or potentially ambiguous title token, do not automatically append an entertainment-domain keyword. Only append a plain entertainment disambiguator keyword (for example: film movie album song novel author director) when the original question itself contains explicit entertainment-role words (e.g., film, movie, album, song, directed, author, novella) or when the question explicitly lists multiple title-like tokens. In those cases, include the visible anchor(s) from summary_1 plus the appropriate entertainment keyword to bias retrieval. If the question lacks entertainment cues and does not list multiple titles, do not guess or add an entertainment disambiguator—keep the query anchored to the visible tokens only.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- When the context contains a concise bridge claim about a named entity and at least one passage provides an explicit, identity-aligned statement linking that same named entity (or a clearly indicated alias) to the asserted relation, append exactly one brief corroborating sentence that paraphrases that passage's linking fact and references the corroborating passage. Perform an explicit identity-alignment check (alias or explicit linking); do not treat mere name co-occurrence or a passage that merely mentions the name as sufficient. Do not append this corroboration if the `context` already contains the identical corroborating fact verbatim. Keep this addition short and keep the overall summary compact.
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.
- When the summaries contain both a short single-token alias and, in the later integrated summary (summary_2), a contiguous multi-token full-name literal that explicitly identifies or is explicitly linked to the same person (for example via an identity link or biographical framing in summary_2), prefer and emit that multi-token full-name literal exactly as it appears in summary_2. Do not prefer the longer form unless summary_2 provides an explicit identity link tying the alias to the full-name literal; if no such explicit link is present, follow the base identity-link rules and do not force selection of the longer form.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: Which district was Miss Universe 2013 held in?
hop1_docs: Miss Universe Spain 2013 | Miss Universe Spain 2013 was the very first edition of the Miss Universe Spain pageant.  It was held in the Convention Center at the Hotel Ada Palace in Madrid on 11 September 2013, exclusively for selecting the Spanish representative to Miss Universe 2013 as a result of the discontinuation of the traditional Miss Spain pageant which was last held in 2011.

Miss Universe Malaysia 2013 | The Miss Universe Malaysia 2013 also referred to as The Next Miss Universe Malaysia 2013 is the 50th edition of the Miss Universe Malaysia pageant which was held at Shah Alam, Selangor on December 10, 2012 where the reigning winner Kimberley Leggett of Penang crowned her successor Carey Ng of Putrajaya.

Miss Universe Malaysia 2014 | The Miss Universe Malaysia 2014 also referred to as The Next Miss Universe Malaysia 2014 is the 51st edition of the Miss Universe Malaysia pageant which was held at the Setia City Convention Centre on December 19, 2013 where the current winner Carey Ng was crowned her successor Sabrina Benett from Perak.  All seventeen contestants from different states will compete for the crown.  Sabrina was represented Malaysia at the Miss Universe 2014.

Miss Universe Thailand 2013 | Miss Universe Thailand 2013, the 14th Miss Universe Thailand pageant, was held at the Royal Paragon Hall, Siam Paragon in Bangkok on May 11, 2013.  The contestants will camp in Krabi.  before flying back to Bangkok for the final stage.  Nutpimon Natthayalak, Miss Universe Thailand 2012, was crowned her successor Chalita Yaemwannang from Bangkok at the end of this event.

Miss Universe 2013 | Miss Universe 2013, the 62nd Miss Universe pageant, was held on 9 November 2013 at the Crocus City Hall, Krasnogorsk, a suburb of Moscow, Russia.  Olivia Culpo of the United States crowned her successor Gabriela Isler of Venezuela at the end of the event.  86 contestants competed for the crown.

Puteri Indonesia 2013 | Puteri Indonesia 2012-2013, the 17th Annual Puteri Indonesia beauty pageant, was held in Jakarta Convention Center, Jakarta, Indonesia on February 1, 2013.  Previous edition of Puteri Indonesia was held on October 2011 and the next edition was supposed to be held on 2012, but because Miss Universe 2012 was held in December 2012 the organization decided to reschedule the event to February 2013.  Thirty eight contestants from all 33 provinces of Indonesia competed for the title of Puteri Indonesia, one of the most prominent beauty pageant titles in the country.  Maria Selena, Puteri Indonesia 2011 from Central Java crowned her successor Whulandary Herman from West Sumatera at the end of this event.  The winner is going to represent Indonesia at the Miss Universe 2013, while the runners-up will represent the nation at the Miss International 2013, Miss Supranational 2013 and Miss Grand International 2013.  The event was broadcast live on Indonesian television network, Indosiar.  The winner of Miss Universe 2012 Olivia Culpo from USA, was present during the event.

Miss Universe Spain 2014 | Miss Spain Universe 2014 was the second edition of the Miss Universe Spain beauty pageant.  It was held on October 28, 2014 at the Teatro Bodevil in the city of Madrid.  Patricia Yurena Rodríguez, Miss Spain Universe 2013, crowned the winner Desirée Cordero Ferrer as her successor who represented Spain in the Miss Universe 2014.
response.summary_1: Passage evidence: "Miss Universe 2013 ... was held on November 9, 2013 at the Crocus City Hall, Krasnogorsk, a suburb of Moscow, Russia." Bridge / missing fact: which administrative district contains Krasnogorsk (i.e., "Which district is Krasnogorsk in?"). Retrieval direction: look up Krasnogorsk (or Crocus City Hall in Krasnogorsk) to find its district name (search query examples: "Krasnogorsk district" or "Crocus City Hall Krasnogorsk administrative district").
hop2_docs: Krasnogorsky District, Moscow Oblast | Krasnogorsky District (Russian: Красного́рский райо́н ) is an administrative and municipal district (raion), one of the thirty-six in Moscow Oblast, Russia.  It is located in the center of the oblast.  The area of the district is 224.99 km2 .  Its administrative center is the city of Krasnogorsk.  Population:  (2010 Census) ; 149,679 (2002 Census);  (1989 Census) The population of Krasnogorsk accounts for 65.0% of the district's total population.

Crocus City Hall | Crocus City Hall (Russian: Крокус Сити Холл ) is a concert hall administratively located in "Krasnogorsk Urban Settlement" (near the city of Krasnogorsk) in Krasnogorsky District, Moscow Oblast (north-west to Moscow Ring Road).

Krasnogorsk, Moscow Oblast | Krasnogorsk (Russian: Красного́рск ) is a city and the administrative center of Krasnogorsky District in Moscow Oblast, Russia, located on the Moskva River, adjacent to the northwestern boundary of Moscow.  Population:  (2010 Census) ;  (2002 Census) ;  (1989 Census)

Krasnogorsk (camera) | The Krasnogorsk-3 (Красногорск-3) is a spring-wound 16mm mirror-reflex movie camera designed and manufactured in the USSR by KMZ.  A total of 105,435 Krasnogorsk-3 cameras were produced between 1971 and 1993.

FC Zorky Krasnogorsk (women) | FC Zorky Krasnogorsk is a Russian women's football team from Krasnogorsk founded in 2006, currently competing in the Russian Championship in its first appearance in the top category.  In 2012 it reached the national cup's final, lost to Zvezda Perm.
Krasnogorsk, Russia | Krasnogorsk (Russian: Красногорск ) is the name of several inhabited localities in Russia.

FC Zorky Krasnogorsk | FC Zorky Krasnogorsk (Russian: ФК «Зоркий» Красногорск ) is a Russian football team based in Krasnogorsk.  It was founded in 1968 and played in the third-tier Soviet Second League from 1968 to 1969 and from 1982 to 1989, participating in amateur competitions in other years.  For the 2017–18 season, it was licensed to play in the third-tier Russian Professional Football League.
response.summary_2: Miss Universe 2013 was held at Crocus City Hall in Krasnogorsk, which is in Krasnogorsky District, Moscow Oblast. Crocus City Hall is administratively located in Krasnogorsky District (passage 2).
